title: Water Sampling and Plating Generations (Antimicrobial Group)
author: Melissa B Duhaime

steps.title: Collect whole water at six sites...
steps.instruction: Collect whole water at six sites using a Van Dorn water collector for deep (~122.5-123.7 m) samples and 2 L containers for surface samples (~15 cm below the surface).

steps.title: Set site names and classifications Sites samples and description 3
steps.instruction: Set site names and classifications Sites samples and description 3. Prefilter whole water using 20μm mesh. 4. Set up peristaltic pump with 3 µm filter 5. Filter approximately 500mL of sample water. 6. Collect the filter in an eppendorf with 1mL of PBS as the particle-associated large fragment

steps.title: Collect the <3μm filtered water as...
steps.instruction: Collect the <3μm filtered water as the free-living (FL) smaller fraction.

steps.title: For the particle-associated (PA) fraction, vortex...
steps.instruction: For the particle-associated (PA) fraction, vortex tube, then partially remove filter with an edge under the closed cap

steps.title: Fully remove filter and resuspend pellet
steps.instruction: Fully remove filter and resuspend pellet

steps.title: Pipette 10μL of both PA and...
steps.instruction: Pipette 10μL of both PA and FL onto separate plates of 4 agar types (TSA, LB, BG11, and LAKE) and spread. This is the parent generation.

steps.title: From the lawns initially plated, by...
steps.instruction: From the lawns initially plated, by sterile loop pick up a single colony.

steps.title: Select a plate of the same media
steps.instruction: Select a plate of the same media.

steps.title: Steak for isolation using a three...
steps.instruction: Steak for isolation using a three phase streak pattern as shown below, sterilizing the loop in between each quadrant. 4. Label these plates "generation 1" 5. Continue until generation 3 is obtained. 6. Use generation 3 for testing.
